shopeeFixer.statusWarnMsg: The label contains large GFA images. This may cause slowness on Shopee.
shopeeFixer.statusErrorMsg: Incompatible GFB bitmap for Shopee detected. Fix before printing.
shopeeFixer.invalidFormatMsg: Incomplete or invalid ZPL code. Make sure to copy the entire original text (must contain ^XA and ^XZ).

result.correct: Label is within Shopee specifications. No correction required.
result.missingDimensions: Missing dimensions. Click Fix to add the correct ^PW and ^LL.
result.wrongDimensions: Wrong dimensions for the selected DPI. Click Fix to adjust.
result.invalidStructure: Invalid ZPL code — missing ^XA or ^XZ. Use the Syntax Validator for full diagnostics.

crossSell.validator: Your ZPL has structural errors. Use the Syntax Validator for complete diagnostics.

zplToPdf.title: ZPL to PDF Converter | ZPLMaster
zplToPdf.h1: Free Online ZPL to PDF Converter
zplToPdf.desc: Convert raw ZPL code into print-ready PDF files in seconds. Supports 4x6 thermal and A4 formats, directly in your browser — no installation required. 100% free and private.

shopeeFix.title: Shopee ZPL Image Fix | ZPLMaster
shopeeFix.desc: Paste your Shopee label ZPL code, the system will diagnose bitmap errors and auto-fix the code with one click. Free and 100% in-browser.

validator.title: ZPL Syntax Validator | ZPLMaster
validator.desc: Analyze your ZPL code and identify structural errors, semantic issues and formatting best practices. Free and 100% in-browser.

labelBuilder.title: ZPL Label Builder | ZPLMaster
labelBuilder.desc: Create transport, shipping and logistics labels without writing code. Generate PDF and ZPL ready for printing.

fix.manualNote: Structural errors (E001, E002, E003) require manual correction.
fix.tooltip: Automatically fixes: ^FD fields without ^FS, negative coordinates and missing dimensions

validator.statusValidMsg: No errors or warnings found. The code is ready to use.

errors.E006: Multiple ^XA detected without ^XZ between them — use Bulk Splitter

warnings.W001: Negative coordinates in ^FO — field may be outside the label
warnings.W004: Commands after ^XZ will be ignored by the printer
warnings.W005: ^PQ0 — zero quantity, nothing will be printed

info.I002: No ^FD fields found — label has no content
info.I003: Encoding not declared — printer will use default

generic.explanation: Review this line structure and adjust the commands according to ZPL syntax.

E001.explanation: ^XA starts the label. Without it, the printer cannot parse the block correctly.

E002.explanation: ^XZ ends the label. Without it, the ZPL is incomplete.

E003.explanation: Order is inverted: ^XA must come first and ^XZ must come last.

E004.explanation: Every field opened with ^FD must be closed with ^FS.

E005.explanation: Barcode commands need data (^FD) and a field close (^FS).

E006.explanation: This file contains multiple labels pasted together. Split them before validating individually.
E006.correct: Split each label into a separate block/file

W001.explanation: Negative coordinates can place content outside printable area.

W004.explanation: Anything after ^XZ is ignored by the printer.

W005.explanation: ^PQ0 sets quantity to zero, so nothing is printed.

I001.explanation: Without ^PW/^LL, the printer uses defaults and label size may be wrong.

labelBuilder.title: ZPL Label Builder
labelBuilder.description: Create professional labels without knowing ZPL
